is a [football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") ground in [Beith](/wiki/Beith "Beith"), Scotland. It was the home ground of [Beith F.C.](/wiki/Beith_F.C. "Beith F.C.") during their time in the [Scottish Football League](/wiki/Scottish_Football_League "Scottish Football League"), and is currently the home ground of [Beith Juniors](/wiki/Beith_Juniors_F.C. "Beith Juniors F.C.").
[Beith Juniors](https://nonleaguescotland.org.uk/beith.html) Non League Scotland

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Football ground in Beith, Scotland

Bellsdale Park

**Bellsdale Park** is a [football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") ground in [Beith](/wiki/Beith "Beith"), Scotland. It was the home ground of [Beith F.C.](/wiki/Beith_F.C. "Beith F.C.") during their time in the [Scottish Football League](/wiki/Scottish_Football_League "Scottish Football League"), and is currently the home ground of [Beith Juniors](/wiki/Beith_Juniors_F.C. "Beith Juniors F.C.").

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Bellsdale_Park&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

Beith moved to Bellsdale Park in 1920, and a 280-seat stand was built on the northern side of the pitch later in the decade.[[2]](#cite_note-UD-2) The club were elected into the new Third Division of the SFL in 1923, and the first SFL match at Bellsdale Park was played on 25 August 1923, a 1–0 win over [Royal Albert](/wiki/Royal_Albert_F.C. "Royal Albert F.C.").[[2]](#cite_note-UD-2)

The ground was twice used as a home ground by other clubs; [Galston](/wiki/Galston_F.C. "Galston F.C.") were forced to play a home match against [Arthurlie](/wiki/Arthurlie_F.C. "Arthurlie F.C.") at Bellsdale Park on 22 March 1924 (a 3–0 win for Arthurlie) after their [Portland Park](/wiki/Portland_Park,_Galston "Portland Park, Galston") ground was unavailable. Arthurlie subsequently played two home matches at Bellsdale Park in 1925 after their [Dunterlie Park](/wiki/Dunterlie_Park "Dunterlie Park") ground was closed due to crowd trouble; a 4–0 win over [Broxburn United](/wiki/Broxburn_United_F.C. "Broxburn United F.C.") on 19 September and a 4–1 win over [East Stirlingshire](/wiki/East_Stirlingshire_F.C. "East Stirlingshire F.C.") on 3 October.[[2]](#cite_note-UD-2)

The Third Division was disbanded at the end of the [1925–26 season](/wiki/1925%E2%80%9326_Scottish_Football_League "1925–26 Scottish Football League"), with the final SFL match at the ground being played on 21 April 1926, a 4–2 defeat by [Leith Athletic](/wiki/Leith_Athletic_F.C. "Leith Athletic F.C."). When Beith were transformed into Beith Juniors in 1938, the new club continued to use the ground.[[2]](#cite_note-UD-2)
